page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but features a high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores all ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, particularly in large-block uncached operations. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-threaded tasks such as Bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang achieve top-tier results. For LLM inference, the instance demonstrates top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, alongside strong text generation throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high resource density and dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, maximizing compute predictability for intensive workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware-accelerated parallel processing. This server is highly suited for high-performance computing (HPC) workloads, large-scale scientific simulations, multi-threaded software compilation, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, representing a tradeoff for storage-heavy or graphics-intensive tasks. However, it excels in compute-heavy benchmarks, achieving top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and highly efficient multi-threaded processing. It is optimized for demanding workloads such as high-performance computing, complex scientific simulations, software compilation, and CPU-based large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing instance with 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.48xlarge features 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps networking for high-performance computing.
tagline: A dedicated 96-core high-performance computing platform with high-speed DDR5 memory and 150 Gbps networking for intensive parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for high-performance computing, simulations, and CPU-based LLM inference
